failure_modes.text: Teams can mistake noise for signal by inventing causes for an extreme result before checking whether anything structural changed.
failure_modes.mitigation: Before assigning causation to an extreme result, check whether the base rate alone could explain the reversion: run a simple pre-post comparison against the historical distribution.

failure_modes.text: Over-reaction to extremes can make the loudest datapoint dominate judgment instead of the longer-run distribution.
failure_modes.mitigation: Dampen response to outliers: weight decisions toward the running average rather than the latest extreme datapoint, and require structural evidence before treating a spike as a trend.

failure_modes.text: Regression-to-the-mean can be misused as a story for inaction when the mean itself has actually shifted downward.
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify the baseline: before invoking regression-to-the-mean as a reason to wait, check whether the mean itself has shifted — inaction is only justified if the long-run distribution is stable.

premortem_questions.text: A change without a reason for the change is not really a change?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "A change without a reason for the change is not really a change?"

premortem_questions.text: What is the base rate or historical likelihood of this extreme performance being sustained?
premortem_questions.source_quote: "What is the base rate or historical likelihood of this extreme performance being sustained?"

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Use the outside view and longer-run distributions before assigning a special causal story to an extreme result.
Separate correlation from causation before crediting a nearby intervention for the result.
Inspect the full range of outcomes, including catastrophe cases, instead of reasoning only from the mean.
heuristics.source_quote: Incorporate simulations to highlight the full range of outcomes possible.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strong on baseline discipline, causal caution, and outside-view checks, so the chunk set stays close to those interventions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor analytics commentary that widened beyond the raw source's stronger signal-vs-noise and structural-change guidance.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses a simulation-oriented mitigation table into a more general full-range-outcomes prompt.

allies: The raw allies section is rich on systems thinking, correlation-vs-causation, and probabilistic thinking for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section explicitly names availability bias, hammer effects, and confirmation bias.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against representativeness-bias, survivorship-bias, and confirmation-bias.
